description:
An aerospace engineer is modeling a tactical Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) based on the RQ-7 Shadow. This requires placing tail booms off the centerline using OpenVSP's XForm and Symmetry tools, and correctly sizing a horizontal stabilizer to mathematically bridge the gap between them.

A geometry specification sheet has been placed on your Desktop (`rq7_shadow_spec.txt`). Read this file to find the required dimensions and locations. Open OpenVSP and build the multi-body aircraft configuration from scratch (Central Pod, Main Wing, Tail Booms, and Horizontal Stabilizer). You must correctly apply spatial translations (XForm) and planar symmetry as described in the spec. Save your completed model to `/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/twin_boom_uav.vsp3`.
